title: [lichen_l2.c:947,1245] get_eui64 pointer used without mutex protection after callback-modifiable state
description: Cross-file analysis: lichen_l2_enable() at line 947 and lichen_l2_iface_init() at line 1245 both call lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() to get a pointer to internal lora_data.eui64, then use that pointer for lichen_link_init(). However, lora_l2.c documents at line 929-933 that 'caller must ensure no concurrent deinit() while using the pointer'. In lichen_l2_enable(), the code holds tx_mutex+rx_mutex but NOT lora_mutex, so a concurrent lichen_lora_l2_deinit() could reinitialize lora_data while the pointer is in use. The deinit would transition state to DEINITING (not RUNNING), so lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() would still return the pointer. The fix is either to copy the eui64 immediately before releasing any locks, or to hold lora_mutex during the use. In practice, the timing window is narrow since deinit requires stop first, but the contract is violated.
close_reason: Fixed: In lichen_l2_enable(), changed to copy EUI-64 to local buffer immediately after get_eui64() call before any further use. Added THREAD SAFETY comment in iface_init() documenting why boot-time path is safe (no concurrency, immediate copy). lichen_link_init() also copies internally via memcpy.
